title: stale queue backlog の再調整方針を board 論点化する
summary: waiting_auth / waiting_manual_review の stale backlog を、単発棚卸しではなく board 裁定済みの triage policy と follow-up 導線に変える候補。

requested_action.target: runtime queue stale backlog reconciliation policy

why_now: auth が正常なのに stale backlog が約60時間放置されており、以後の heartbeat でも再燃しやすい。今 board に上げれば、低リスクな triage 規則・closure 規則・reporting 規則として再利用可能な形にできる。
expected_benefit: ゆうすけの手動トリアージ負担を減らし、queue 健全性の可視化と backlog 再発防止を durable な運用ルールとして残せる。
possible_harm: 性急に drain すると本来再開すべき案件を誤クローズする恐れがあるため、自動適用せず board review で guardrail を先に確定する必要がある。

reversibility.rollback_path: まずは board review 用の方針化のみ。実行 directive を出さなければ既存 runtime には変更なし。

root_issue: stale queue backlog に対する board-approved triage / closure / reopen ルールがまだ無く、auth recovery 後も queue が自動で健全化しない。
desired_change: board で backlog triage policy を定義し、safe-close / reopen / escalate の分岐基準と follow-up artifact を決める。
risk_hints: user-facing 変更ではないが execution backlog の誤処理リスクあり
auth root change ではなく queue triage governance の論点
先に board review、後で directive の順が適切

title: Create a dedicated Queue Triage Analyst path for dominant-prefix backlog
summary: Queue telemetry is stable enough that repeated supervisor-core review loops are lower leverage than a named triage owner with a runbook.
why_now: waiting_auth and waiting_manual_review remain dominant, and the current telemetry is not changing the remediation shape.
expected_benefit: Reduce review repetition, shorten backlog remediation, and keep supervisor-core focused on escalation.
possible_harm: If queue shape changes, a dedicated role can become over-specialized; mitigation is an explicit escalation fallback.

reversibility.rollback_path: Reassign dominant-prefix triage back to supervisor-core and disable the dedicated analyst path.

metrics: dominant prefixes stabilized instead of moving

root_issue: Dominant-prefix queue telemetry keeps recurring without an ownerized triage path, so supervision loops are becoming repetitive.
desired_change: Move dominant-prefix remediation to a dedicated Queue Triage Analyst flow with checklist, owner, and success criteria.

title: Require bundle manifest + dry-run sync before live runtime reflection
summary: Partial file-by-file reflection between workspace and live runtime is unsafe because the live side still depends on an older contract surface.
why_now: The live runtime reflection audit found contract drift and missing support files, so partial sync is too risky.
expected_benefit: Prevent contract breakage, make live reflection reproducible, and keep changes bundle-scoped.
possible_harm: Slower reflection turnaround and extra manifest overhead.

reversibility.rollback_path: Stop bundle sync and keep runtime reflection on a dry-run-only path until the manifest is ready.

metrics: missing live files: handoff_contract.py, project-injection-registry.json, task_runtime.py
bundle-level manifest + dry-run copy is the safe reflection unit

root_issue: Live runtime reflection is still vulnerable to contract drift when changes are mirrored file-by-file.
desired_change: Treat runtime reflection as a bundle with manifest, dry-run diff, and backup gates before live sync.

title: Promote anomaly-delta monitor contract across heartbeat, board, and scorecard prompts
summary: Routine heartbeat and scorecard output is still drifting toward narrative review; the stable pattern is signal_only for routine runs and agenda_candidate only for anomalies, deltas, and precedent gaps.
why_now: The 2026-03-25 prompt diff and board hardening notes define a monitor-style contract, but enforcement is still uneven.
expected_benefit: Reduce routine governance noise, keep Board rare, and make anomaly handling predictable.
possible_harm: If over-applied, it can suppress useful narrative context or hide weak signals.

reversibility.rollback_path: Restore the prior narrative-style prompt blocks if the monitor contract proves too strict.

metrics: no decision_record / no directive / no direct user report

root_issue: Heartbeat and scorecard outputs still mix routine digest with governance reasoning, which makes Board noise harder to control.
desired_change: Standardize anomaly-delta monitor prompts so routine runs emit signal_only and only anomalies become agenda candidates.

title: heartbeat governance snapshot shows board_touch_high and exploration_drift on repeated stale-backlog follow-up
summary: 24h snapshot shows 2 heartbeat runs, candidate_rate 0.5, candidate_to_board_touch_ratio 2.0, board_touch_high warning, and exploration_drift_risk=true while board_overload/scout_saturation/duplicate_spike remain false.
root_issue: Routine heartbeat cycles are still producing board touches without a signal-only digest baseline, so the monitor is oscillating on the same stale backlog follow-up instead of staying delta-only.
desired_change: Tighten the anomaly-delta monitor contract: keep healthy cycles as digest signal_only, and emit agenda_candidate only when a new metric delta or threshold breach appears.
why_now: The current snapshot shows board_touch_high and exploration_drift on a tiny 2-run window; repeating the same Board touch would add noise without new evidence.
expected_benefit: Reduce board noise, preserve signal-only digest behavior, and make repeated health checks easier to interpret.
possible_harm: If tuned too aggressively, a genuine recurring anomaly could be delayed; keep the threshold change reversible and observation-only.

reversibility.rollback_path: Restore the previous monitor threshold / emission rule; no runtime directive required.

title: Split supervisor-core and route dominant-prefix triage to Queue Triage Analyst
summary: Supervisor-core で重複しやすい観測・triage・品質レビューを分離し、dominant-prefix triage は専任経路へ切り出す。
why_now: 同じ supervisor 論点が複数レポートで反復しており、追加観測より triage 変換がボトルネックになっている。
expected_benefit: 重複レポートを減らし、owner と次アクションを早く固定できる。
possible_harm: 分離しすぎると supervisor-core が横断文脈を失う可能性がある。

reversibility.rollback_path: triage を supervisor-core に戻し、Queue Triage Analyst を advisory-only にする。

metrics: 20/21 成功でも監督系の重複が残る
複数レポートが dominant-prefix triage を示唆

root_issue: supervisor-core is still absorbing triage work that should be specialized
desired_change: dominant-prefix triage should move to a dedicated Queue Triage Analyst path

title: Keep routine board / heartbeat / scorecard signal-only and reserve candidates for anomalies
summary: 定常時の board / heartbeat / scorecard は digest に固定し、candidate 化は anomaly delta があるときだけに絞る。
why_now: 直近の scorecard は新規 anomaly がなく、候補を増やすと Board noise が増えるだけだから。
expected_benefit: 審議密度を下げ、真正の anomaly に Board の時間を残せる。
possible_harm: 閾値を高くしすぎると、遅い drift を見落とす可能性がある。

reversibility.rollback_path: signal-only 制約を外し、従来どおり candidate を許可する。

metrics: agent-scorecard-review 20260326-0600 は no anomaly / no candidate
loop health は安定寄りだが重複が多い

root_issue: routine monitoring is producing too much governance noise
desired_change: steady-state monitoring should remain signal-only unless there is a real anomaly delta

title: Require exact-target preflight plus owner/due/success criteria before light-edit or scout handoff escalation
summary: 軽微修正と scout / PoC の引き渡しを、対象確認と owner/due/success criteria が揃うまで上げない。
why_now: exact target mismatch と PoC handoff stall が別々の報告で再発しており、前段のガードが不足している。
expected_benefit: retry cost を下げ、曖昧な handoff を減らし、次アクションを実行可能にする。
possible_harm: 小さな作業でも前段確認が増え、速度が少し落ちる。

reversibility.rollback_path: preflight を外し、従来の軽い handoff に戻す。

metrics: pharma-free-tools の軽微修正で exact target mismatch が発生
sidebiz / research-analyst で owner/due/success criteria の欠落が繰り返し指摘

root_issue: light edits and scout handoffs are still missing a mandatory preflight gate
desired_change: targets, owners, due dates, and success criteria should be confirmed before escalation

title: Direct board runtime write validation candidate
summary: Validate that board_runtime direct write and assemble paths still function when heartbeat mapping is bypassed.
why_now: Heartbeat mapping path showed schema mismatches and direct board path must be verified independently.
expected_benefit: Confirms whether the defect is isolated to heartbeat mapping or broader runtime writes.
possible_harm: Minor ledger noise from a single synthetic test item.

reversibility.rollback_path: ignore or prune synthetic test records later

root_issue: heartbeat to board mapping may be broken
desired_change: verify direct board write path separately

title: Standardize safe-close records with mandatory owner / next_action / success_criteria / review_after / linked_evidence
summary: safe-close / reopen の分岐自体はあるが、記録の必須項目が薄く、silent failure と reopen drift を運用上で見分けにくい。close record を構造化して監査可能にする。
why_now: board-auditor の audit で、safe-close の silent failure 防止条件と reopen 条件がまだ抽象的で、close 後の監視チェックも policy から見えにくいと確認できた。
expected_benefit: close / reopen の責務を明確化し、同じ案件の誤クローズと再開遅延を減らせる。
possible_harm: close 時の記録項目が増え、運用者の入力コストが少し上がる。

reversibility.rollback_path: required fields を外して、従来の free-form close note に戻すだけで巻き戻せる。

metrics: safe-close の silent failure 防止条件が定量化されていない
reopen 条件が広く、再開の発火点が曖昧
close 後の監視チェックが policy からはまだ見えにくい

root_issue: safe-close / reopen decisions are structurally valid but operationally under-specified, so different operators can read the same close record differently.
desired_change: close records should carry explicit owner, next_action, success_criteria, review_after, and linked_evidence fields.

title: Canonicalize board-cycle artifact freshness and slot verification
summary: board-agenda-assembly, premeeting brief, dispatch verification, and dispatch result can disagree on whether a cycle artifact is present because they read different latest views and publish at different times.
why_now: the 20260326-1035 cycle produced a live contradiction between freshness checks and dispatch verification while the slot-specific board artifacts themselves were already fresh.
expected_benefit: eliminate stale-vs-missing ambiguity, reduce false-negative verification, and make board-cycle status auditable.
possible_harm: one more canonical snapshot step adds overhead and can slow reporting slightly.

reversibility.rollback_path: keep the current per-artifact checks and remove the canonical freshness layer if it adds too much overhead.

metrics: seed / precheck / brief align on slot=20260326-1035
board-dispatch-verification still reports premeeting=NG and dispatch=未確認

root_issue: different board-cycle monitors can disagree about artifact freshness because there is no canonical slot registry or publish-state contract.
desired_change: read board-cycle artifacts from a canonical snapshot with explicit states: published, pending, stale, missing, and observed-late.

title: Define exec-agent dispatch delivery states for safe-temporary-file fallback and live receipt
summary: exec agents are still dispatched through safe temporary files because live receive is unavailable, but the runtime has no explicit contract for receipt, pending_artifact, or completion semantics.
why_now: the 20260326-1035 dispatch repeated the safe temporary file fallback across exec roles and live receipt remained unobserved.
expected_benefit: make completion semantics explicit, reduce false assumptions about live acceptance, and make verification easier.
possible_harm: slightly more verbose dispatch artifacts and a temporary sense of extra process overhead.

reversibility.rollback_path: continue the current safe-temporary-file fallback and ignore the explicit receipt states if they prove too heavy.

metrics: board 系 dispatch: accepted / completed / artifact confirmed
exec 系 dispatch: safe temporary file fallback

root_issue: execution-agent dispatch is operating with an implicit fallback and no canonical receipt semantics.
desired_change: make dispatch artifacts declare delivery mode, expected receipt type, and follow-up state transitions for pending_artifact / completed / blocked.

title: Canonicalize board-cycle freshness into a single slot/state contract
summary: The 20260326-1235 cycle is fresh in the seed / precheck / premeeting brief, but board dispatch verification still reads the same cycle through a different freshness lens. That creates stale-vs-missing ambiguity even when the artifacts exist.
why_now: The same board slot is simultaneously treated as ready and unresolved across different monitors, so the cycle can look both fresh and incomplete.
expected_benefit: Make published / pending / stale / missing / observed-late states deterministic, reduce false-negative verification, and make Board-cycle status auditable.
possible_harm: Adds one canonical snapshot step and slightly more reporting overhead.

reversibility.rollback_path: Keep the current per-artifact checks and remove the canonical freshness layer if it adds too much overhead.

metrics: dispatch verification still reports exec live result unresolved
different monitors read different freshness states

root_issue: Different board-cycle monitors can disagree about artifact freshness because there is no canonical slot registry or publish-state contract.
desired_change: Read board-cycle artifacts from a canonical snapshot with explicit states: published, pending, stale, missing, and observed-late.

title: Define exec-agent dispatch delivery states for safe-temporary-file fallback and live receipt
summary: Exec agents are still dispatched through safe temporary files because live receive is unavailable, but the runtime has no explicit contract for receipt, pending_artifact, or completion semantics.
why_now: The 20260326-1035 dispatch repeated the safe temporary file fallback across exec roles and live receipt remained unobserved.
expected_benefit: Make completion semantics explicit, reduce false assumptions about live acceptance, and make verification easier.
possible_harm: Slightly more verbose dispatch artifacts and a temporary sense of extra process overhead.

reversibility.rollback_path: Continue the current safe-temporary-file fallback and ignore the explicit receipt states if they prove too heavy.

metrics: board dispatch: accepted / completed / artifact confirmed
exec dispatch: safe temporary file fallback

root_issue: Execution-agent dispatch is operating with an implicit fallback and no canonical receipt semantics.
desired_change: Make dispatch artifacts declare delivery mode, expected receipt type, and follow-up state transitions for pending_artifact / completed / blocked.

title: Standardize safe-close records with mandatory owner / next_action / success_criteria / review_after / linked_evidence
summary: Safe-close and reopen policy exists, but the record contract is still under-specified between auditors, runbooks, and queue notes. Different operators can therefore read the same close record differently.
why_now: Board-auditor and supervisor-core both point at the same gap: the policy exists, but the operational record still lacks the fields that make reopen decisions auditable.
expected_benefit: Close / reopen decisions become easier to audit, silent failures fall, and reopen drift is reduced.
possible_harm: Close-time notes become slightly more structured and the operator input cost increases a little.

reversibility.rollback_path: Remove the required fields and return to the existing free-form close note.

metrics: policy already defines safe-close / reopen / hold / escalate
runbook view still misses due/evidence/stop conditions
close record fields remain weakly fixed

root_issue: Close records are structurally valid but operationally under-specified, so the same close can be interpreted differently later.
desired_change: Close records should carry explicit owner, next_action, success_criteria, review_after, and linked_evidence fields.

title: Split board dispatch completion from exec live receipt in reporting
summary: Board-side send/accept can finish even when the execution-side live result is still pending. The current reporting model risks marking the run as complete too early, so live receipt and artifact confirmation need their own status.
why_now: The latest postmeeting dispatch closed cleanly on the Board side, but the exec live receipt remains unresolved. This is a repeated precedent gap, not a one-off reporting typo.
expected_benefit: Status reports will stop overstating completion, and operators will be able to tell whether the remaining work is a send/accept issue or a live execution issue.
possible_harm: Adds one more status state and can create short-term confusion if the old completion flag is not mapped carefully during migration.

reversibility.rollback_path: Keep the legacy completion flag, revert only the live-receipt split, and continue recording the old status alongside the new one during rollback.

signals: board-postmeeting dispatch reached all Board agents without failures
exec live result still only shows pending_artifact
freshness gate was stable but reporting still collapses distinct completion states

root_issue: board-side delivery success is being treated as if it were the same thing as exec-side live completion
desired_change: separate send/accept from live receipt / artifact-confirmed status in board reporting and runbooks

title: Separate triage from security audit and boundary/DDS review
summary: Recent board, CEO, and auditor artifacts all point to the same unresolved governance boundary: backlog triage is stable only when security audit and DDS/boundary review are kept on a separate lane with explicit sequencing.

requested_action.target: separate triage from security audit and DDS/boundary review

why_now: The same separation is now repeated across premeeting input, postmeeting memo, and auditor acceptance, but it is still not codified as a distinct operating lane with clear handoff boundaries.
expected_benefit: Reduces scope drift, keeps triage conclusions crisp, and prevents security evidence from being mixed into backlog closure decisions.
possible_harm: If codified too loosely, the audit lane could inherit triage shortcuts or blur the manual-review boundary.

reversibility.rollback_path: Remove the separate audit-lane wording and restore the prior combined triage note.

metrics.review_order: triage -> audit -> boundary review -> DDS impact

signals: triage and security audit are converging on a strict separation pattern
security audit and DDS impact are explicitly kept out of the triage lane

title: Split review-approved and apply-blocked states in board reporting
summary: A proposal can be review-approved yet still blocked at apply time by a protected path or routing boundary, but the current runtime/reporting surface does not make that split explicit enough.

requested_action.target: approval/apply state split in board runtime

why_now: The 22:45 dispatch record shows a proposal that is approved in review but blocked on apply because of protected-path boundaries, which creates a status gap that can be misread as completion.
expected_benefit: Board, execution, and audit layers will be able to distinguish policy approval from mutable-path success, reducing reporting ambiguity and preventing overstatement of completion.
possible_harm: If over-modeled, the split can add another status that operators must learn; the contract should stay minimal and map cleanly to existing blocked states.

reversibility.rollback_path: Collapse the explicit split back into the existing blocked state mapping in reporting and templates.

root_issue: Review approval and apply-time blocking are not surfaced as distinct runtime states, so a protected-path block can look like a completed handoff.
desired_change: Make review-approved/apply-blocked a first-class explicit state in board reporting, handoff logs, and follow-up templates.

title: Require proof-path before declaring completion in board and workspace reports
summary: The latest learning review shows that report text can imply completion before primary evidence, spot-check, and evidence memo are attached; this gap spans board, execution, and docs reporting.
why_now: workspace-report-learning-review-20260327-0300 elevated proof-path and state separation as reusable learnings, but current report templates still allow completion language to outrun evidence.
expected_benefit: Reduce false done claims, keep report verification auditable, and make apply/review/manual_required/effect-confirmed distinctions harder to collapse.
possible_harm: Adds a small verification step and may slow optimistic status updates.

reversibility.rollback_path: Remove the proof-path gate and return to current reporting templates.

metrics: board-side completion remains split from exec live receipt

root_issue: Completion language is still being used before evidence is attached, which can make partial progress read like done.
desired_change: Require proof-path evidence before completion claims are treated as done across board and workspace reports.

title: Investigate phantom proposal generation pipeline — 5 proposals reported but not found on filesystem across 2+ consecutive cycles
summary: Self-improvement verification reports 5 new proposals (user-reporting-enhancement, agent-coordination-enhancement, agent-performance-optimization, autonomy-loop-health, governance-optimization) that do not exist on the filesystem. This has persisted across at least 2 consecutive verification cycles, suggesting a broken generation/saving step in the proposal pipeline.

requested_action.target: self-improvement proposal generation and persistence pipeline

why_now: Phantom proposals have persisted for 2+ cycles. The self-improvement verification sweep now counts them as not_found, inflating the total proposal count and undermining trust in the governance pipeline's artifact reliability.
expected_benefit: Identifies whether the issue is in generation (agent hallucinated completion), persistence (write failed silently), or reporting (wrong path referenced). Either way, restoring artifact integrity improves verification reliability.
possible_harm: Investigation requires reading proposal generation logs and may be noisy if the root cause is a one-off transient failure that self-healed.

reversibility.rollback_path: No changes needed for investigation-only action.

root_issue: Proposal generation pipeline produces completion signals without actually persisting proposal files, creating phantom artifacts that undermine verification integrity.
desired_change: Identify and fix the generation/persistence gap so that proposal completion implies file existence.

title: Enforce user notification policy on cron jobs — token-management and completion-checker still set to delivery.mode announce
summary: User explicitly instructed (v6 policy) that token management notifications should be OFF except mode switches. However, 3 cron jobs (token-management-snapshot, token-management-evaluation, claude-code-completion-checker) remain configured with delivery.mode: 'announce', sending unwanted Telegram notifications every 15 minutes and 3 minutes respectively.

requested_action.target: cron job delivery.mode configuration vs user notification policy

why_now: The mismatch between explicit user instruction and actual cron configuration has been identified but not yet corrected. The noise from these notifications undermines the 4x/day reporting cadence policy.
expected_benefit: Eliminates unwanted notification spam, aligns cron configuration with user communication preferences, and restores trust in policy enforcement.
possible_harm: Setting delivery.mode to 'none' means these jobs become completely silent unless they also have internal logging. Mode-switch notifications from token management need a separate delivery path.

reversibility.rollback_path: Restore delivery.mode to 'announce' on the affected jobs.

root_issue: User notification policy is documented in memory but not enforced in cron job delivery configuration.
desired_change: Set delivery.mode to 'none' on token-management-snapshot, token-management-evaluation, and claude-code-completion-checker. Ensure mode-switch notifications have a separate delivery mechanism.
